hotspots.title: Manuel Adorni’s illicit enrichment scandal
hotspots.blurb: Argentina’s Chief of Staff Manuel Adorni faces intense scrutiny over alleged illicit enrichment, with revelations of $14 million in unreceipted furniture purchases and $245,000 cash payments. President Javier Milei’s unwavering support for Adorni amid calls for his resignation has deepened internal government tensions and eroded public trust.
hotspots.affect: This scandal commands disproportionate attention due to its moral outrage framing, cross-factional political contention, and the perceived hypocrisy of austerity policies juxtaposed with lavish personal spending.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise in Germany and internal conflicts
hotspots.blurb: Germany’s far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) leads polls in Saxony-Anhalt with 41% support, ahead of the September 2026 state election. The party’s surge is fueled by public dissatisfaction with the government, while internal conflicts, such as nepotism allegations and legal battles over donations, continue to dominate coverage.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to alarmist framing over the AfD’s potential to secure an absolute majority, polarized contention over its policies, and moral panic over its impact on German democracy.

hotspots.title: Trump’s Iran war escalation and diplomacy
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration’s military campaign against Iran, dubbed 'Operation Epic Fury,' has entered a critical phase, with mixed signals on ceasefire negotiations. Trump’s threats to resume bombing if Iran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz have heightened global tensions, while diplomatic efforts by Secretary of State Marco Rubio to mend ties with the Vatican and Italy add complexity to the narrative.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to its alarmist framing of potential war escalation, polarized international reactions, and moral stakes over civilian casualties and global security.

hotspots.title: Javier Milei’s governance and scandals
hotspots.blurb: Argentine President Javier Milei’s administration faces mounting challenges, including plummeting approval ratings, internal cabinet rebellions, and economic turmoil. The Manuel Adorni scandal has overshadowed Milei’s libertarian reforms, while his international trips and confrontations with governors and opposition figures fuel perceptions of instability.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to its moral outrage framing over corruption allegations, polarized contention between Milei’s supporters and critics, and alarmist narratives about Argentina’s economic collapse.

hotspots.title: Vox’s electoral surge and internal crises
hotspots.blurb: Spain’s far-right Vox party is making significant gains in regional elections, particularly in Andalusia, while facing internal defections and controversies. The party’s 'Spaniards first' policy and confrontations with the PP over coalition agreements have dominated coverage, alongside allegations of racism and historical revisionism.
hotspots.affect: This hotspot is charged due to its polarized contention over immigration policies, moral outrage over racist rhetoric, and alarmist framing of Vox’s potential to reshape Spanish politics.
